summary: Draft, closed-unmerged PR focused mainly on src/melee/ty/tylist.c and ty trophy-list types/prototypes. The diff rewrites TyList code toward typed TyListArg/TyListState access, JObj helper calls, natural loops, local float literals, and archive/string-table offset access. Bot report shows two new tylist function matches and several improvements, but also multiple unmatched-item regressions including tylist functions and .data/.sdata sections.

key_files.role: Primary decomp/matching rewrite for trophy-list code; adds local structs, helper-based JObj access, loop rewrites, literal/offset changes, and function body reshaping.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: src/melee/ty/tylist.c +579/-730, 16 hunks
raw_diff: tylist.c hunks replacing expanded JObj translate/dirty code with HSD_JObjSetTranslateY
raw_diff: tylist.c hunks replacing string literals with un_803FDD18 offsets
bot report: matches un_8031263C and un_803124BC; regressions un_8031305C, fn_80313BD8, un_80312BAC, un_80312904, .data, .sdata

key_files.role: Ty trophy-list type refinement; changes TyListArg fields to arrays for links, jobjs, and texts, and adjusts several TyListState field types/pads.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: TyListArg x0/x4/x8 -> links[3], xC/x10/x14 -> jobjs[3], pad_18 -> texts[3]
raw_diff: TyListState pointer fields changed from struct TyListArg* to TyListArg* and pad/type adjustments

key_files.role: Prototype cleanup for recovered TyList signatures.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: un_80312904(void*, s8) -> un_80312904(struct TyListArg* arg, s8 arg1)
raw_diff: un_8031305C signature reduced to struct TyListState* state, s8 arg2
raw_diff: removed fn_80313BD8 prototype and renamed fn_8031438C parameter to gobj

key_files.role: Incidental formatting-only compound literal spacing change.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: GXColor casts changed from (GXColor){...} to (GXColor) {...}
changed_files.jsonl: +3/-3, 1 hunk

key_files.role: Incidental formatting-only function-pointer cast spacing change.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: function pointer casts changed from u8(*)(...) to u8 (*)(...)
changed_files.jsonl: +3/-3, 1 hunk

key_files.role: Incidental formatting-only callback function-pointer cast spacing change.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: cb cast changed to s32 (*)(HSD_GObj*, s32*) spacing
changed_files.jsonl: +1/-1, 1 hunk

what_changed: tylist.c gained local helper/temporary structs such as TyListArchiveAnimData, TyListWaitData, TyListCameraState, TyListDispEntry, and TyListDispState.
TyListArg was made more structured in types.h by grouping repeated pointer slots as links[3], jobjs[3], and texts[3].
Several tylist functions were rewritten from generated-looking raw-field, goto, while, and expanded assert/dirty code into typed field access, helper calls, and for loops.
un_80312904 changed from a void* row-style implementation using TyListRow and external float symbols to TyListArg* access with jobjs/texts arrays and inline float constants.
un_80312BAC was simplified with a single backward index loop, state->entryCount loop, entry->links[1] traversal, HSD_JObjSetTranslateY, and archive->jobjs animation loop.
un_80312E88 gained an inline helper that iterates over jobjs and applies HSD_JObjSetTranslateY for non-null, non-index-2 jobjs.
Archive symbol names in un_803124BC and un_8031263C were changed from string literals to offsets from un_803FDD18.
tylist.h prototypes were updated to typed TyListArg/TyListState signatures and one local/static function prototype was removed.
Small unrelated gm/gr files only changed cast/compound-literal spacing.

smart_moves.text: Recovering TyListArg as arrays for repeated link, JObj, and text slots enabled source to express repeated indexed behavior directly instead of separate x0/x4/xC/x10/x14/text fields.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: types.h TyListArg links[3], jobjs[3], texts[3]
raw_diff: tylist.c uses arg->jobjs[i], arg->texts[0..2], entry->links[1]

smart_moves.text: Expanded JObj translate/dirty/assert sequences were collapsed to HSD_JObjSetTranslateY where the operation was clearly setting translate.y.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: un_80312BAC replaces manual jobj->translate.y plus dirty logic with HSD_JObjSetTranslateY
raw_diff: un_80312E88_inline uses HSD_JObjSetTranslateY

smart_moves.text: Repeated pointer/animation logic was expressed as for loops over fixed counts, making the likely repeated structure clearer.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: un_80312BAC for (i = 0; i < 3; i++) archive->jobjs[i]
raw_diff: un_80312E88_inline for (i = 0; i < 3; i++)

decomp_lessons.lesson: When adjacent struct fields form repeated slots, recovering them as arrays can unlock cleaner loops and clearer access patterns.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: TyListArg links[3]/jobjs[3]/texts[3]
raw_diff: tylist.c loops over jobjs and accesses texts by index

decomp_lessons.lesson: Expanded baselib JObj translate/dirty code can often map back to HSD_JObjSetTranslateY when the source operation is only setting translate.y.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: un_80312BAC and un_80312E88_inline helper replacements

decomp_lessons.lesson: A code-match PR can still be risky when string/literal/data rewrites improve text symbols but regress .data or .sdata; keep data-section changes narrowly justified.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: string literals changed to un_803FDD18 offsets
bot report: .data regression 20.07% -> 4.40%, .sdata regression 70.27% -> 63.41%

decomp_lessons.lesson: Prototype updates should accompany recovered function signatures so callers and headers stop carrying void* or stale parameter shapes.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: un_80312904 signature changed to TyListArg*
raw_diff: un_8031305C prototype changed

naming_conventions.convention: Use short role names for locals when roles are clear: state, entry, jobj, parent_jobj, pos_x, pos_y, pos_z, show, archive.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: un_80312904 and un_80312BAC local variable changes

naming_conventions.convention: Keep unknown struct members address-style or xNN while grouping proven repeated roles into neutral arrays like links, jobjs, and texts.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: TyListArg keeps x24/x25/x28/x2C/x30 while adding links[3], jobjs[3], texts[3]

naming_conventions.convention: For HSD_GObj parameters, use gobj when the role is only the generic object.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_8031438C(HSD_GObj* arg0) -> fn_8031438C(HSD_GObj* gobj)

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Replace expanded JObj setter/assert/dirty sequences with HSD_JObjSetTranslateY helper calls.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: un_80312BAC helper replacements
raw_diff: un_80312E88_inline helper
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Requires objdiff/build evidence for final acceptance; this PR's bot report still shows regressions in nearby tylist functions.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use natural for loops for fixed-count JObj animation/update arrays instead of while/goto generated shapes.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: for loops over i < 3 in un_80312BAC and un_80312E88_inline
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Loop form improved readability, but the loaded evidence does not prove final acceptance because the PR was closed unmerged.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use typed temporary structs to describe archive/camera/display state offsets while broader types are still incomplete.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: TyListArchiveAnimData, TyListCameraState, TyListDispState definitions
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Temporary structs and padding need validation against current headers and object layout before promotion.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Replacing named string literals with base+offset string-table references can affect data matching and should be treated as high-risk data work.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: TyDatai.dat/TyDatai.usd and symbol-name literals changed to data + offsets
bot report: .data and .sdata regressions
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Conflicts with current standards preferring inline string literals unless data ownership evidence is explicit.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Repeated TyList slots as arrays
accepted_candidate_records.text: In tylist-style structs, consecutive link/JObj/text slots may be clearer as fixed arrays when code repeatedly indexes them; this can enable natural loops and reduce raw xNN field churn.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: TyListArg links[3], jobjs[3], texts[3]
raw_diff: tylist.c uses arg->jobjs[i], arg->texts[n], entry->links[1]

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use JObj setter helper for translate.y cleanup
accepted_candidate_records.text: When generated code expands translate.y assignment plus dirty/assert handling, HSD_JObjSetTranslateY may be the intended source-level helper.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: un_80312BAC and un_80312E88_inline

source_update_candidates.title: String-table offset rewrites should be treated as data ownership changes
source_update_candidates.text: This PR changed ordinary archive and symbol-name string literals in tylist to un_803FDD18 + offsets while the bot report showed .data and .sdata regressions. Curator may want to cross-link this PR as supporting evidence for existing standards that discourage string-literal-to-symbol/data-offset rewrites without explicit data ownership evidence.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: TyDatai.dat/TyDatai.usd and tyInitModelTbl-style literals replaced with data + offsets
text_corpus bot report: .data and .sdata regressions
source_update_candidates.reason: The evidence supports a cautionary standard update/cross-reference, not acceptance of the PR's data-offset style.

source_update_candidates.title: tylist.c contains trophy-list UI state and entry structures
source_update_candidates.text: PR evidence shows src/melee/ty/tylist.c and src/melee/ty/types.h working with TyListState and TyListArg for trophy-list UI entries, including entry links, JObjs, text objects, selectedIdx, entryCount, and trophy index fields.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: TyListArg and TyListState hunks
raw_diff: un_80312904, un_80312BAC, un_80312E88 usages
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path fact for curator review; not promoted because the PR was closed unmerged and some changes regressed.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the PR's string-literal-to-un_803FDD18-offset changes as accepted style; bot evidence includes data-section regressions and current standards caution against such changes without ownership evidence.
Do not claim these functions were finally matched or merged; loaded metadata says merged_at null and the PR was closed.
Do not infer reviewer intent because no human reviews or review comments are present.
